codeReview.summary: FileLockManager is properly wired into Orchestrator and ExecutionController. Locks are acquired before task execution using enriched_files and released on completion/failure/cancellation. Deadlock prevention is implemented via atomic all-or-nothing lock acquisition - if any lock conflicts, the task gets NO locks and fails immediately, preventing hold-and-wait conditions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The commit ID in the handoff (92404d0fe2ccabf5a40f7d9c60a8da3efb1c5e47) does not exist in the repository. The actual commit that introduced file_locks.rs is 3205d00. This discrepancy should be investigated - either the handoff used a different branch or the commit was amended/rebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git log shows 3205d00 for file_locks.rs, but handoff referenced 92404d0

summary: The orch-file-locks feature is properly implemented with FileLockManager integrated into the Orchestrator. File locks are acquired on enriched_files before task execution and released on completion/failure/cancellation. Deadlock prevention is achieved via atomic all-or-nothing acquisition (no partial locks). All 28 file lock unit tests and 9 integration tests pass. MockLlm is only used in test modules, not production code. Minor pre-existing clippy warnings exist but are unrelated to this feature.
